---
title: "Lincoln, Gettysburg, and the Nature of War"
subtitle: "A public working-paper package"
description: "How the Gettysburg Address turns soldiers' deaths into an obligation to preserve the nation and seek a new birth of freedom."
---

## Research question

> How does the Gettysburg Address transform the deaths of soldiers into a sacred obligation to preserve and refound the nation, and what does this reveal about Lincoln's understanding of the nature of war?

This project has produced an approved public working-paper package on Abraham
Lincoln's wartime rhetoric. It examines how the Gettysburg Address relates
soldier death, national survival, democratic obligation, and political rebirth
while testing those interpretations against Lincoln's other writings,
historical context, and disconfirming evidence.

::: {.callout-important title="Publication status"}
The working paper is public with disclosed limitations. It is not a
journal-final, quotation-final, or limitation-free publication. Several
quotation-final checks remain before a stronger publication claim should be
made.
:::

## Site Guide

::: {.site-map}
<div>

### Working Paper

Read the public working paper or download the PDF.

- [Working paper page](working-paper.html)
- [PDF working paper](outputs/paper.pdf)

</div>

<div>

### Workflow

See a plain-language summary of how the project moved from question to sources,
evidence, claims, reviews, and publication.

- [How the project worked](workflow.html)
- [Detailed methodology paper](methodology-paper/methodology-paper.html)

</div>

<div>

### Evidence

Browse a compact evidence table or download the full CSV appendix.

- [Evidence table](evidence.html)
- [Evidence appendix CSV](outputs/evidence-appendix.csv)

</div>

<div>

### Reviews

Read the individual audit and review results that narrowed the final claims.

- [Review index](reviews/)
- [Publication approval review](reviews/publication-approval-review.html)

</div>

<div>

### Reports

Inspect publication readiness and reproduction details.

- [Publication audit](publication-audit.html)
- [Reproducibility report](reproducibility-report.html)

</div>
:::

## Workflow Summary

This site is organized around a traceable research workflow. The working paper
is the public argument, but the site also exposes the evidence table, review
results, publication audit, reproducibility report, and methodology paper that
show how that argument was built and checked.

For a reader-friendly overview, start with
[How the Project Worked](workflow.html). For the formal methodological argument,
read [The Scholarly Build System](methodology-paper/methodology-paper.html).

## Publication package

The public package includes the working paper, its publication audit, a
reproducibility report, and the evidence appendix used to check claims against
the source-grounded matrix.

- [Publication audit](publication-audit.html): citation coverage, quotation
  cautions, source boundaries, claim coverage, review coverage, output checks,
  and the release approval status.
- [Reproducibility report](reproducibility-report.html): Python, pytest,
  Quarto, render commands, test results, evidence counts, and reproduction
  steps for the working-paper package.
- [Evidence appendix CSV](outputs/evidence-appendix.csv): 32 evidence rows
  exported from the evidence matrix, including passages, observations,
  interpretations, alternative readings, confidence, and review status.

## Individual Reviews

The review suite tests the paper from multiple angles rather than treating one
audit as enough. The [review index](reviews/) links to historical, rhetorical,
theological-language, source and citation, countervoice, anachronism,
disconfirmation, coding, draft-source, and publication-approval reviews.

- [Browse all audit and review results](reviews/)
- [Open the evidence table](evidence.html)

## Methodology working paper

The methodology paper has also been approved for public release as a working
paper with disclosed limitations. It evaluates the governed human-AI workflow
used in this project as a single developmental case study.

- [Read the methodology working paper](methodology-paper/methodology-paper.html)

::: {.callout-note title="Methodology status"}
The methodology paper is public as a working paper. It is not journal-final,
universally validated, or independent replication evidence. It preserves
limits around single-case scope, developmental-case bias, process-logging
effects, accepted-only intervention records, documentation burden, and the
difference between repository validation and interpretive validity.
:::

## Method

The workflow separates source text, observation, inference, and conclusion. It
preserves manuscript variants, records contradictory evidence, and
distinguishes Lincoln's public rhetoric from claims about private belief.
Primary sources are preferred for claims about Lincoln's language and actions.
The reader-facing overview is available at
[How the Project Worked](workflow.html).

The staged process covers corpus acquisition, evidence coding, analysis and
thesis review, paper drafting, adversarial review, publication-package audit,
and human approval for release as a public working paper. Project data,
validation tooling, and committed publication artifacts are available in the
[public GitHub repository](https://github.com/ashitaka-emishi/lincoln-war-research-project).

## Current state

The historical track has completed corpus acquisition, evidence coding,
analysis and thesis review, drafting, adversarial review, and final
publication-package preparation. The scholar approved the Goal 60 package for
release as a public working-paper package with disclosed limitations.

The methodology track has completed case-study evaluation, methodology-paper
drafting, adversarial methodology review, and publication-readiness review. The
scholar approved the methodology paper for release as a public methodology
working paper with disclosed limitations.

Known limits remain visible in the publication package:

- the Bliss Copy transcription should receive a second manuscript-image check
  before quotation-final use;
- Everett pamphlet quotations should be checked against page images because
  the normalized text is OCR-derived;
- the Harrisburg *Patriot and Union* criticism remains a Dickinson derivative
  transcription pending original newspaper image review;
- noisy newspaper OCR is used only for limited reception framing;
- secondary-source engagement remains intentionally limited and should be
  broadened before journal submission.
